_comment: Canonical Epic 3 detection-loop output shape per SPEC §15. orion-e3a (this bookend) pins the contract; orion-e3f extends the smoke test to assert it end-to-end. Each invariant is a shell-verifiable check the future automated drill runs against the actual detection_runs + detection_findings rows after a 3-tick fixture drill.

expected_findings.rationale: http.Client constructed without an explicit timeout field. Fires once per matching constructor; the fixture's NewClient function returns one such constructor.
http.Get invoked without a retry/backoff library mention in the negation window. FetchUpstream calls http.Get with no wrapper.
if err != nil { return nil } pattern drops error context. LoadConfig in errors.go has this exact inline form.

Heuristic side-effect: rvl-cli fires this on any .go file that imports net/http and makes outbound calls without a known circuit-breaker library import. Deterministic across runs on this fixture. Documented as ancillary, not primary, so future matcher additions do not silently break the bookend.

dedup_signature_invariants._comment: Per SPEC §8.3: dedup_signature must be deterministic across runs given identical input. Two ticks against an unchanged fixture must produce identical signatures so the cross-reference phase short-circuits the autofile.
dedup_signature_invariants.stable_across_runs: SELECT count(DISTINCT dedup_signature) FROM detection_findings WHERE fixture = 'epic3-detection' AND tick IN (1, 2) GROUP BY slug HAVING count(*) <> 2 -> 0 rows
dedup_signature_invariants.stable_across_processes: Re-invoking the loop in a fresh process against the same SHA produces the same signature for each (slug, file, line) tuple

three_tick_drill._comment: The 3-tick drill orion-e3f wires the live smoke against. Asserts dedup, quiescence, and detection-on-churn behavior end-to-end.

tick_1.rationale: Initial scan finds all 3 fixture gaps; autofile creates one tracker issue per finding.

tick_2.rationale: Dedup short-circuits — same findings already tracked. Phase depends on §15.3.1: quiescent if eligible backlog drained, otherwise completed with zero new findings.

tick_3.rationale: Operator introduces one new gap (extra file in fixture). Cross-reference recognizes 3 existing + 1 new; autofile creates 1 issue.

detection_run_invariants._comment: Asserted by orion-e3f against the detection_runs table after the 3-tick drill.
detection_run_invariants.row_count: SELECT count(*) FROM detection_runs WHERE binding_id = $ORION_BINDING_ID -> 3
detection_run_invariants.all_completed_or_quiescent: SELECT count(*) FROM detection_runs WHERE binding_id = $ORION_BINDING_ID AND phase NOT IN ('completed', 'quiescent') -> 0
detection_run_invariants.monotonic_started_at: Started_at strictly increasing across the 3 rows

provenance_breakdown._comment: Per SPEC §15.4 / §21.3. Self-referential-loop guard counts processed issues by provenance. New customer (this fixture is fresh) so customer-filed count is zero.
provenance_breakdown.loop_guard_suppression_reason: first-3-runs suppression per SPEC §15.4 final paragraph

risksink_behavior._comment: Per SPEC §15.3 and orion-e35. Risksink emits each new finding to Polaris's risk register; local fallback queues to risksink_pending when Polaris is unreachable.
risksink_behavior.polaris_reachable: POST /api/v1/risks called once per new finding with origin='orion-detection'; risksink_pending row count = 0
risksink_behavior.polaris_unreachable_or_unconfigured: risksink_pending row count = 3 after tick 1; LoopDriver tick still exits 0

progressive_disclosure_cap._comment: Per SPEC §15.2 phase 5 and orion-e36. Cap is min(scan.max_auto_filed_per_run, detection.backlog_target_depth - current_eligible_count). For the 3-finding fixture with default config (max=25, target=20, current=0), cap returns 3 (no truncation).

loopguard_behavior._comment: Per SPEC §15.4 and orion-e37. Self-referential warning is suppressed for the first 3 detection_runs on a binding. The 3-tick drill stays inside that suppression window, so the warning MUST NOT fire.
loopguard_behavior.evaluation_rule: Orion-filed > 3 × Customer-filed for 3 consecutive runs over ≥30 days; suppressed for new bindings (< 3 runs)
